title: _host.py is file-loaded and exec_module'd twice per hook invocation across every entry script — the run(host,...) parameter is dead
evidence: Every entry script's __main__ guard does `sys.exit(run(hostapi.load_host()) or 0)` — an eager, explicit call to `hostapi.load_host()`, which does `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` + `module_from_spec` + `spec.loader.exec_module(mod)` on `_host.py` beside it (hostapi.py:183-189). That loaded `host` is then passed into `run(host, argv=None)`, but every `run()` in the audited scripts (pre-write.py:153-160, pre-edit.py, pre-bash.py, post-write-edit.py, security-pass.py, migration-pass.py, pre-read.py, session-start.py, git-enforce.py, taskwrite.py, and the rest sharing the same generated pattern) discards the `host` argument entirely and just calls `main()`/`main(argv)` — e.g. pre-write.py:153-160: `def run(host, argv=None): main(); return 0`. `main()` in turn calls `project_root()`/`get_host()` (imported from `_hooklib`), and `_hooklib.get_host()`'s process-level cache (`_HOST`) is empty on this first internal call, so it invokes `hostapi.load_host()` a SECOND time — re-doing the same file-path resolution, `importlib.util` spec build, and `exec_module` of `_host.py` that the `__main__` guard already did one line earlier in the same process.
impact: Every single hook invocation across the whole hook surface (both the ca and ca-codex plugins, ~19 entry scripts) pays the file-load + exec_module cost of `_host.py` twice instead of once. Per-call cost is modest (a small already-cached file, tens to a couple hundred lines of Python to parse/exec), so this is a low-severity redundancy rather than a latency cliff — but it is universal (every EXEC/WRITE/EDIT/session-start hook call on the user's synchronous critical path) and easy to eliminate, and the dead `host` parameter suggests the seam was designed for the loaded instance to flow through but the wiring was never finished.
recommendation: Have `run(host, argv=None)` actually use the passed-in `host` — either call `_hooklib.set_host(host)` (a small new setter) before invoking `main()`, or thread `host` explicitly into `main()`'s signature — so `get_host()`'s process cache is pre-seeded and the second `hostapi.load_host()` call inside `main()` never fires.
acceptance_criteria: A single hook process invokes hostapi.load_host() (i.e. execs _host.py) exactly once, verifiable by a call-count assertion/trace
No change to any hook's pass/fail verdict under either host
